source_dialogue.speaker: Veda
source_dialogue.text: Hi, Father. I'm Veda. I'm new to the area and was curious about your church. Could you tell me a little bit about it?

source_dialogue.speaker: Priest
source_dialogue.text: Of course, Veda. Our church is based on the teachings of Jesus Christ. We believe in loving our neighbor and treating others as we would want to be treated. We strive to live according to Christ's example and teachings.

source_dialogue.speaker: Veda
source_dialogue.text: That sounds like a really great way to live. I can see why so many people are drawn to this religion. What do you think makes Christianity different from other religions?

source_dialogue.speaker: Priest
source_dialogue.text: Well, there are a lot of different interpretations of Christianity, but for us, it's all about following Jesus Christ's example. He was a man who loved unconditionally and forgave easily. He preached compassion and understanding, even for those who disagreed with him or did not follow his teachings perfectly. We try to emulate that in our own lives."

source_dialogue.speaker: Veda
source_dialogue.text: That does sound different. I know that forgiveness is a big part of Christianity, but it's not always easy to do. How do you think Christians are able to forgive so easily?

source_dialogue.speaker: Priest
source_dialogue.text: Well, I think it comes from our belief that everyone is capable of change and redemption. We believe that no one is beyond hope or help, and so we are always willing to forgive those who have wronged us. It's not always easy, but it is something we strive for.

scene.narrative_en: Veda thought about going to church because she was interested in the religion. She had never been to church before, but she had heard good things about it. She decided to go to a nearby church and see what it was like.

source_dialogue.speaker: Rylea
source_dialogue.text: Hey Shavon, what's up? You seem troubled.

source_dialogue.speaker: Shavon
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, I am. I'm just having a hard time and needed someone to talk to.

source_dialogue.speaker: Rylea
source_dialogue.text: Of course, man. I'm always here for you. What's going on?

source_dialogue.speaker: Shavon
source_dialogue.text: It's just everything. Work is stressing me out, my relationship is falling apart, and I feel like I'm losing touch with my friends. I don't know what to do.

source_dialogue.speaker: Rylea
source_dialogue.text: Well, let's start with work then. What's going on there?

source_dialogue.speaker: Shavon
source_dialogue.text: It's just that everything is so demanding and I can't keep up. I'm constantly behind and it feels like I'm never going to catch up.

source_dialogue.speaker: Rylea
source_dialogue.text: Okay, that does sound pretty tough. But it sounds like you're doing the best you can under the circumstances. Maybe you just need to cut yourself some slack and focus on one thing at a time. As for your relationship, what's going on there?

source_dialogue.speaker: Shavon
source_dialogue.text: We're just fighting all the time and it feels like we're growing apart instead of together. I don't know if we can make it through this rough patch.

source_dialogue.speaker: Rylea
source_dialogue.text: Do you still love her?

source_dialogue.speaker: Shavon
source_dialogue.text: Yes, I do. But it's just so hard right now.

source_dialogue.speaker: Rylea
source_dialogue.text: Then you should definitely try to work things out. It sounds like you're going through a lot right now, but your friends are still here for you. So don't forget that.

Hesitant, uses filler words, expresses worry informally.

source_dialogue.speaker: Jabriel
source_dialogue.text: Hi there! Are you looking for someone to take care of your pet while you're out of town?

source_dialogue.speaker: Client
source_dialogue.text: Yes, I am. I'm going on vacation for a week and I need someone to watch my cat.

source_dialogue.speaker: Jabriel

source_dialogue.speaker: Jabriel

source_dialogue.speaker: Client
source_dialogue.text: Alright, I'll be leaving on Saturday and I'll be back next Saturday. Can you come over on Friday evening to meet my cat and get everything set up?

source_dialogue.speaker: Jabriel

source_dialogue.speaker: Cornell
source_dialogue.text: I know what you're thinking, Dontrell. I can sense your feelings and know exactly what you're going to say or do next. You're thinking about how much you miss your family, and how you wish you could see them again. You're wondering if there's anything you can do to make that happen. And you're also feeling a little guilty, because you know that if you could see them again, it would only be for a short while before they died.

source_dialogue.speaker: Dontrell
source_dialogue.text: Cornell, how can you possibly know all that? It's like you can read my mind!

source_dialogue.speaker: Cornell
source_dialogue.text: I told you, I can sense your feelings. It's like I have a connection to your thoughts and emotions. I'm not sure why or how it works, but ever since we met, I've been able to understand what you're thinking and feeling pretty easily.

source_dialogue.speaker: Dontrell
source_dialogue.text: Wow...that's amazing! And a little bit scary too. But it does explain why we've always been so close - even though we've only known each other for a few months.

source_dialogue.speaker: Cornell
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, I guess our connection is pretty strong. But don't worry, Dontrell - I'll never use it to manipulate or control you in any way. I promise."

source_dialogue.speaker: Dontrell
source_dialogue.text: That's a relief! I was starting to feel a little bit like you could read my mind, and it was kind of creepy. But if you promise not to use your abilities to control me, then I'm okay with it.

source_dialogue.speaker: Cornell
source_dialogue.text: I swear, Dontrell. I'll never use my abilities to control or manipulate you in any way. You have my word.

speakers.speech_style_notes: Measured, reassuring tone with occasional pauses.

scene.narrative_en: Cornell knows what Dontrell is thinking. He can sense her feelings and know exactly what she is going to say or do next.

source_dialogue.speaker: Arizona
source_dialogue.text: I find it amusing that the events of the world are so far removed from my own life.

source_dialogue.speaker: Wife
source_dialogue.text: What do you mean?

source_dialogue.speaker: Arizona
source_dialogue.text: Well, take this news story for example. It's about a conflict in some country that I've never even heard of. What does it have to do with me?

source_dialogue.speaker: Wife
source_dialogue.text: I'm not sure. What do you think it has to do with you?

source_dialogue.speaker: Arizona
source_dialogue.text: Probably nothing. But it's just interesting to think about how much is happening in the world that I have no connection to whatsoever.

source_dialogue.speaker: Wife
source_dialogue.text: I guess that's true. But even if you're not directly affected by something, it can still be important to know about it.

source_dialogue.speaker: Arizona
source_dialogue.text: Why?

source_dialogue.speaker: Wife
source_dialogue.text: Well, for one thing, it helps you understand the world better. And also, if something bad happens in another country, it could have an indirect effect on you. For example, if there's a war going on somewhere, that could cause the price of oil to go up, and that would affect everyone.

source_dialogue.speaker: Arizona
source_dialogue.text: Hmm, I see your point. I hadn't thought of it like that.

source_dialogue.speaker: Wife
source_dialogue.text: So even though these events may seem far removed from your life, they can still have an impact on you in ways that you might not expect.

speakers.speech_style_notes: Measured, reflective, uses full sentences with a calm tone.

scene.narrative_en: Arizona was sitting in his armchair, listening to the news on the radio. He found it amusing that the events of the world were so far removed from his own life.

source_dialogue.speaker: Tressa
source_dialogue.text: Hi, can I ask you a question?

source_dialogue.speaker: Teacher
source_dialogue.text: Of course! What would you like to know?

source_dialogue.speaker: Tressa
source_dialogue.text: How long does it usually take for someone to become fluent in a new language?

source_dialogue.speaker: Teacher
source_dialogue.text: Well, that really depends on the person. Some people pick up new languages very quickly, while others take a bit longer. There are a lot of factors that can affect how fast someone learns a new language, such as motivation, intelligence, and prior knowledge.

source_dialogue.speaker: Tressa
source_dialogue.text: Hmm, that's interesting. Do you think there's anything I can do to help me learn faster?

source_dialogue.speaker: Teacher
source_dialogue.text: Definitely! There are lots of things you can do to help yourself learn more quickly. For example, studying regularly is important. Try to set aside some time each day to review what you've learned. You might also want to try using flashcards or other memory aids to help remember vocabulary words. Additionally, listening to native speakers and practicing speaking as much as possible will also be beneficial.

speakers.speech_style_notes: Patient, explanatory, uses clear, supportive language.

scene.narrative_en: Tressa was interested in learning. She started making progress by studying for a few hours each day. Within a month, she could speak fluently and was able to understand most of what was said to her.

source_dialogue.speaker: Curtis
source_dialogue.text: I was just wondering... do you have any plans to retire soon? Or sell the bakery?

source_dialogue.speaker: Mr. Saunders
source_dialogue.text: Oh, I hadn't really thought about it. To be honest, I'm not sure what I would do without this place. It's been my life for so long.

source_dialogue.speaker: Curtis
source_dialogue.text: I see. Well, I was just wondering because I would be interested in buying the bakery from you when you're ready to retire.

source_dialogue.speaker: Mr. Saunders
source_dialogue.text: Oh, Curtis. I don't know what to say. That's a very generous offer. Let me think about it and get back to you, okay?

source_dialogue.speaker: Curtis
source_dialogue.text: Okay, Mr. Saunders. Thanks for considering it.

speakers.speech_style_notes: Polite, slightly hesitant, uses indirect phrasing.

scene.narrative_en: Curtis has worked at the bakery for four years. He has learned every aspect of the business and believes he could run it himself. Curtis has been saving money and plans to purchase the bakery from the current owner in two years.

mapped_refs.notes: Korean term for a bakery/confectionery shop

source_dialogue.speaker: Naydelin
source_dialogue.text: I'm not going back to that school. I hate it.

source_dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
source_dialogue.text: Why do you keep getting kicked out?

source_dialogue.speaker: Naydelin
source_dialogue.text: I don't know. I just don't like it there, that's all. And all the kids are phony.

source_dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
source_dialogue.text: What's a phony?

source_dialogue.speaker: Naydelin
source_dialogue.text: You know, someone who's not real, who puts on an act. All the kids at my school are phonies. They're all trying to be something they're not. It's all a big act.

source_dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
source_dialogue.text: But what are you trying to be?

source_dialogue.speaker: Naydelin
source_dialogue.text: I don't know...I'm just myself, I guess. But everyone else is always putting on an act and it drives me crazy.

source_dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
source_dialogue.text: You're not going back to school and you don't know what you want to do with your life. Is that it?

source_dialogue.speaker: Naydelin
source_dialogue.text: I guess so. Yeah, pretty much.

source_dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
source_dialogue.text: Well, what are you going to do?

source_dialogue.speaker: Naydelin
source_dialogue.text: I don't know. Maybe just travel around or something. See the country.

speakers.speech_style_notes: Defiant, uses blunt statements and repeats key phrases.

scene.narrative_en: Naydelin develops the reputation of being a rebel. He gets into fights and is constantly getting kicked out of school. He doesn't care what people think of him and he wants to be seen as an adult.

mapped_refs.notes: 가짜는 학교에서 ‘가짜’ 혹은 ‘가짜 애’로 쓰이며, ‘phonies’와 같은 의미다.

source_dialogue.speaker: Sade
source_dialogue.text: I'm telling you, man, she is just too good for me. I don't deserve her.

source_dialogue.speaker: Friend
source_dialogue.text: Dude, stop being so negative. You're a great guy and she clearly loves you.

source_dialogue.speaker: Sade
source_dialogue.text: I don't know...I just feel like I'm not good enough for her. She's so amazing and I'm just...I don't know.

source_dialogue.speaker: Friend
source_dialogue.text: Sade, you are an amazing guy and she loves you. Just relax and enjoy it.

source_dialogue.speaker: Sade
source_dialogue.text: I guess you're right. I'm just being too hard on myself.

source_dialogue.speaker: Friend
source_dialogue.text: Exactly. Now go enjoy your amazing girlfriend. You deserve it!

speakers.speech_style_notes: Reflective, slightly hesitant, uses informal contractions.
Encouraging, upbeat, uses casual slang like "dude".

scene.narrative_en: Sade was having a conversation with his friend. He felt proud of himself because he was able to hold an intelligent conversation.

mapped_refs.notes: Informal vocative used like “hey” or “man” in casual speech.
informal vocative equivalent to 'dude'

source_dialogue.speaker: Calum
source_dialogue.text: I can't believe it's still raining.

source_dialogue.speaker: Mother
source_dialogue.text: I know, dear. It's been raining for days now.

source_dialogue.speaker: Calum
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, I know. I just wish it would stop already.

source_dialogue.speaker: Mother
source_dialogue.text: Well, I don't think it will be stopping anytime soon.

source_dialogue.speaker: Calum
source_dialogue.text: Ugh, I really don't like the rain.

source_dialogue.speaker: Mother
source_dialogue.text: Yes, it can be quite unpleasant can't it?

speakers.speech_style_notes: Speaks with a slightly whiny, exasperated tone, using short clipped sentences.
Speaks gently with a soothing, maternal tone, using polite tag questions.

scene.narrative_en: Calum finds that the rain did not go. Now Calum gets wet as she steps outside and feels the cold droplets fall on her head and run down her face.

source_dialogue.speaker: Yamir
source_dialogue.text: I can't believe I agreed to do this. I'm already so behind on everything else.

source_dialogue.speaker: Lily
source_dialogue.text: What's wrong? You seem really stressed out.

source_dialogue.speaker: Yamir
source_dialogue.text: I am. I feel like I can never catch up. Every time I turn around, there's something else that needs to be done. And it's all because I keep taking on too much.

source_dialogue.speaker: Lily
source_dialogue.text: Why do you keep doing that?

source_dialogue.speaker: Yamir
source_dialogue.text: I don't know. I guess I just feel like if I don't, then I'm not really doing my part or contributing enough. But it's just too much sometimes and it makes me so stressed out.

source_dialogue.speaker: Lily
source_dialogue.text: Well, maybe you need to start saying no more often. Just because someone asks you to do something doesn't mean you have to say yes. You have a right to say no and set your own limits.

source_dialogue.speaker: Yamir
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, maybe you're right...I should start saying no more often. It's just so hard though when people are counting on me and asking for favors all the time. But maybe if I start setting some boundaries, it'll help reduce my stress levels in the long run. Thanks for talking with me about this, Lily - it really helped put things into perspective!

speakers.speech_style_notes: Calm, supportive, offers gentle advice, uses reflective questioning.

scene.narrative_en: Yamir is a high school student who often takes on too much work. She frequently signs up for more extracurricular activities than she can handle and regularly agrees to do favors for her friends, even when she's already overwhelmed with other commitments. As a result, Yamir is constantly stressed out and feels like she can never catch up.

source_dialogue.speaker: Adiel
source_dialogue.text: Hi, I was just checking my account balance and it's missing $200. Can you tell me what happened?

source_dialogue.speaker: Teller
source_dialogue.text: Let me take a look. When did you last check your balance?

source_dialogue.speaker: Adiel
source_dialogue.text: Yesterday.

source_dialogue.speaker: Teller
source_dialogue.text: And how much was in your account then?

source_dialogue.speaker: Teller
source_dialogue.text: Okay, let me check to see if there have been any recent transactions. (After a few moments) There was a withdrawal of $200 from an ATM on Main Street yesterday afternoon. Do you remember using that ATM?

source_dialogue.speaker: Adiel
source_dialogue.text: No, I didn't use it yesterday. Someone must have stolen my card and used it without my knowledge. Is there any way to get my money back?

source_dialogue.speaker: Teller
source_dialogue.text: We can refund the money to you, but it will take a few days for the transaction to be reversed. In the meantime, we can cancel your card and issue you a new one.

source_dialogue.speaker: Adiel
source_dialogue.text: That would be great, thank you.

source_dialogue.speaker: Teller
source_dialogue.text: No problem, we'll take care of it right away.

speakers.speech_style_notes: Polite but urgent, uses clear questions.

scene.narrative_en: Adiel discovers the loss when she goes to check her bank account balance and sees that the $200 is missing. She immediately contacts her bank to see if they can refund the money.

source_dialogue.speaker: Emmaleigh
source_dialogue.text: Maria, I'm feeling really sick. I have a high fever and a rash all over my body. I'm so weak and tired, all I want to do is just rest.

source_dialogue.speaker: Maria
source_dialogue.text: Oh no, Emmaleigh! You have the measles! You need to go to the doctor right away!

source_dialogue.speaker: Emmaleigh
source_dialogue.text: I don't think that's necessary. I'll just drink lots of fluids and rest, and I'll be fine in no time.

source_dialogue.speaker: Maria
source_dialogue.text: Emmaleigh, you could get very sick from the measles. It's best to err on the side of caution and see a doctor just to be sure. Plus, they can give you medication to help make you more comfortable.

source_dialogue.speaker: Emmaleigh
source_dialogue.text: Maria, I appreciate your concern, but I really don't think seeing a doctor is necessary. I'll be fine, I promise.

source_dialogue.speaker: Maria
source_dialogue.text: Well, if you're sure that's what you want to do, then I'll support you. But please just take care of yourself and rest as much as possible.

speakers.speech_style_notes: Soft-spoken, hesitant, prioritizes rest over medical intervention.

scene.narrative_en: Emmaleigh has the measles and is feeling very sick. He has a high fever and a rash all over his body. He is weak and tired, and all he wants to do is rest.

mapped_refs.notes: 병원은 의료 서비스를 제공하는 일반적인 장소를 의미한다

source_dialogue.speaker: Rahim
source_dialogue.text: Hey True, how are you today?

source_dialogue.speaker: True
source_dialogue.text: I'm doing well, Rahim. How about you?

source_dialogue.speaker: Rahim
source_dialogue.text: I'm good. So, what have you been up to lately?

source_dialogue.speaker: True
source_dialogue.text: Not much. Just hanging out with friends and family. You know, the usual stuff.

source_dialogue.speaker: Rahim
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, I know what you mean. So, do you have any plans for the future?

source_dialogue.speaker: True
source_dialogue.text: Well, I'm still in school right now so I'm just focusing on that for the time being. After I graduate though, I'd like to travel a bit and see different parts of the world. There's so much to see and experience out there and I just want to make the most of it while I can.

source_dialogue.speaker: Rahim
source_dialogue.text: That sounds amazing! I've always wanted to travel myself but haven't had the opportunity yet. Hopefully one day we can both travel together and check things off our bucket lists!

speakers.speech_style_notes: Enthusiastic, asks open‑ended questions, uses informal but polite tone.
Polite, shares personal plans, uses straightforward informal speech.

scene.narrative_en: Rahim talks to True because Rahim is interested in his life and enjoys his company. True makes her laugh and feel comfortable, so she wants to be around him more.

source_dialogue.speaker: Jazz
source_dialogue.text: I'm really glad you gave me the opportunity to lead the team in your absence. It's something I've been wanting to do for a while now.

source_dialogue.speaker: Boss
source_dialogue.text: Well, you certainly proved yourself today. You did a great job leading the team and handling everything that came up. I'm impressed.

source_dialogue.speaker: Jazz
source_dialogue.text: Thank you, I appreciate that. I really wanted to show you that I'm capable of leading and managing the team effectively.

source_dialogue.speaker: Boss
source_dialogue.text: You definitely did that today. You showed excellent leadership skills and made sure everything ran smoothly. I'll be happy to recommend you for future leadership opportunities.

scene.narrative_en: Jazz gets a chance to prove herself when she is asked to lead the team in the absence of the manager. Jazz wants to prove that she is capable of leading the team and show her skills. She successfully leads the team and proves herself to be a competent leader.

source_dialogue.speaker: Vicki
source_dialogue.text: I just feel so trapped, man. I can't even begin to tell you how unhappy I am right now.

source_dialogue.speaker: Alex
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, I can imagine. It's been tough for you lately.

source_dialogue.speaker: Vicki
source_dialogue.text: It feels like everything is just falling apart and there's nothing I can do about it. I'm just stuck here, living off my parents and barely scraping by.

source_dialogue.speaker: Alex
source_dialogue.text: Have you looked into getting a job?

source_dialogue.speaker: Vicki
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, I've been applying to places but it's been months and I haven't had any luck. And even if I did get a job, it wouldn't be enough to get me out of this hole I'm in.

source_dialogue.speaker: Alex
source_dialogue.text: Well, what about going back to school? You could get a degree and then look for a better paying job.

source_dialogue.speaker: Vicki
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, but that would take years and I don't know if I can wait that long. Plus, I don't even know if that would guarantee me a good job after all that time and money spent on school.

source_dialogue.speaker: Alex
source_dialogue.text: Have you considered moving out of your parents' place?

source_dialogue.speaker: Vicki
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, I've thought about it but I can't afford it on my own. And even if I could find a roommate, I don't know if I could handle living with someone else right now.

source_dialogue.speaker: Alex
source_dialogue.text: Well, what do you want to do then? Just stay here and be unhappy for the rest of your life?

source_dialogue.speaker: Vicki
source_dialogue.text: No, of course not. But it feels like no matter what I do, I'm just stuck in this rut and I don't know how to get out of it.

source_dialogue.speaker: Alex
source_dialogue.text: Vicki, you're not going to be stuck in this forever. Things will get better, you just have to be patient and keep trying. Everything will work out in the end, trust me.

speakers.speech_style_notes: Speaks slowly, with frequent pauses and self‑deprecating remarks.
Speaks calmly, uses supportive phrasing and occasional rhetorical questions.

scene.narrative_en: Vicki is unhappy because he feels trapped. He has been unemployed for months and has been struggling to make ends meet. He's been forced to move back in with his parents and feels like he's never going to be able to get his life back on track.

mapped_refs.notes: A common Korean female name used similarly to address someone for encouragement.
Korean speakers naturally refer to their parents as 부모님.

source_dialogue.speaker: Mahnoor
source_dialogue.text: Wow, I can't believe I built this all by myself! It looks amazing.

source_dialogue.speaker: Sarah
source_dialogue.text: You did a great job, Mahnoor. This building is really impressive.

source_dialogue.speaker: Mahnoor
source_dialogue.text: Thanks, Sarah. I'm pretty proud of it. It took a lot of hard work, but it was worth it.

source_dialogue.speaker: Sarah
source_dialogue.text: I can imagine. How long did it take you to build it?

source_dialogue.speaker: Mahnoor
source_dialogue.text: Oh, about a month. I worked on it every day after school and on weekends.

source_dialogue.speaker: Sarah
source_dialogue.text: That's a really long time. I don't think I could've been that patient.

source_dialogue.speaker: Mahnoor
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, it was tough at times. But like I said, it was worth it in the end.

speakers.speech_style_notes: Excited, enthusiastic, uses first-person boastful statements.

scene.narrative_en: Mahnoor constructs a building. The building she creates is tall and imposing, with lots of windows to let in natural light. It's clear that Mahnoor is proud of her accomplishment; she stands back to admire her handiwork with a satisfied smile on her face.

mapped_refs.notes: common Korean female name used in similar address
Common Korean female name used when thanking someone.

source_dialogue.speaker: Kenzie
source_dialogue.text: I just feel so stuck in my job. I've been doing the same thing for years and I just can't seem to move up or get out.

source_dialogue.speaker: Best Friend
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, I can definitely understand how you feel. It's tough when it feels like you're stuck in a rut. But sometimes, it can be helpful to look at your situation from a different perspective. Maybe there are things you could be doing differently in your job that would help you to move up or get out?

source_dialogue.speaker: Kenzie
source_dialogue.text: I don't know, I've tried everything I can think of. And even if there were things I could be doing differently, I don't know if it would make any difference. It just feels like no matter what I do, I'm not going to get anywhere.

source_dialogue.speaker: Best Friend
source_dialogue.text: Well, it's worth considering anyway. And even if it doesn't work out the way you want it to, at least you tried something different and made an effort to change your situation. That's always better than feeling stuck and hopeless. So why don't you give it some thought and see if anything comes to mind? Who knows, maybe something will come up that you hadn't considered before that could really help you out!

source_dialogue.speaker: Kenzie
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, I guess you're right. It's worth a shot. Thanks for the talk, it really helped me to see things in a different light.

source_dialogue.speaker: Best Friend
source_dialogue.text: No problem, anytime!

speakers.speech_style_notes: Speaks hesitantly, uses personal pronouns, expresses frustration.
Speaks reassuringly, uses supportive language, asks reflective questions.

scene.narrative_en: Kenzie was talking to his best friend about how he felt stuck in his job. His best friend listened patiently and then offered some advice that helped Kenzie see his situation in a new light. Now Kenzie feels motivated to make a change.

source_dialogue.speaker: Tenley
source_dialogue.text: Hey, Sarah. What's up?

source_dialogue.speaker: Sarah
source_dialogue.text: Hey! Not much, what about you?

source_dialogue.speaker: Tenley
source_dialogue.text: Same here. Just getting ready for school. I don't really like having to think about what to wear every day, so I just stick to the same outfit.

source_dialogue.speaker: Sarah
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, I can understand that. I'm always trying to find new outfits and it's just a lot of work. Plus, it's hard to find something that looks good and is also comfortable.

source_dialogue.speaker: Tenley
source_dialogue.text: Exactly! I don't want to have to worry about whether my clothes are going to be uncomfortable or if they're going to look good. I just want to be able to put on the same thing every day and know that it will work.

source_dialogue.speaker: Sarah
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, that would be nice. But sometimes it's fun to mix things up and try new styles. Have you ever tried wearing something different?

source_dialogue.speaker: Tenley
source_dialogue.text: No, not really. I mean, I'll switch out my shoes sometimes or wear a different color shirt, but nothing too drastic. I like knowing what I'm going to wear each day. It makes getting dressed in the morning a lot easier.

source_dialogue.speaker: Sarah
source_dialogue.text: I can see that. But it must get kind of boring wearing the same thing all the time.

source_dialogue.speaker: Tenley
source_dialogue.text: Not really. I don't really care what other people think about my clothes. I'm comfortable with what I'm wearing and that's all that matters to me.

speakers.speech_style_notes: Speaks methodically, prefers straightforward explanations, low affect.

scene.narrative_en: Tenley is a high school student who likes to be consistent. She always wears the same clothes to school so that she doesn't have to worry about what to wear in the morning. Tenley's favorite outfit is a black t-shirt and jeans with her Converse sneakers.

source_dialogue.speaker: Anastazia
source_dialogue.text: Hey, Sasha. How are you doing?

source_dialogue.speaker: Sasha
source_dialogue.text: I'm doing alright. Just been a little stressed lately. School is just really tough right now.

source_dialogue.speaker: Anastazia
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, I can imagine. But you're so smart, I'm sure you'll get through it no problem. You always seem to find a way to succeed no matter what the challenge is.

source_dialogue.speaker: Sasha
source_dialogue.text: Thanks, Anastazia. That means a lot coming from you. You're just always so confident and everything seems to come so easily to you. I wish I could be more like that.

source_dialogue.speaker: Anastazia
source_dialogue.text: To be honest, it's not all rainbows and butterflies like it may seem from the outside looking in. I have my own struggles and insecurities too, we all do. It's just a matter of not letting them get the best of us and continuing to push forward no matter what life throws our way

source_dialogue.speaker: Sasha
source_dialogue.text: You're right. We all have our own battles to fight. But it's good to know that even someone like you who seems to have it all together still has to deal with the same issues as everyone else.

source_dialogue.speaker: Anastazia
source_dialogue.text: Exactly. No one is perfect. We're all just human beings doing the best we can with what we have.

scene.narrative_en: Anastazia was born into a wealthy family and has always had everything he could ever want. He is smart, good-looking, and athletic, and he's never had to work a day in his life. Things just seem to always go his way.

source_dialogue.speaker: Mariely
source_dialogue.text: I can't believe I lost the winning ticket. I'm so angry right now.

source_dialogue.speaker: Lottery Official
source_dialogue.text: I'm sorry to hear that, Mariely. What can I do to help you?

source_dialogue.speaker: Mariely
source_dialogue.text: There is nothing you can do. I had the winning ticket and now it's gone.

source_dialogue.speaker: Lottery Official
source_dialogue.text: Well, let's see if we can retrace your steps and see if we can find it. When did you last have it?

source_dialogue.speaker: Mariely
source_dialogue.text: The last time I remember having it was when I bought it yesterday morning.

source_dialogue.speaker: Lottery Official
source_dialogue.text: Okay, so we'll start there. Do you remember where you bought it?

source_dialogue.speaker: Mariely
source_dialogue.text: Yes, at the grocery store on Main Street.

source_dialogue.speaker: Lottery Official
source_dialogue.text: Great, we'll go there first then. Maybe someone turned it in or found it there.

source_dialogue.speaker: Mariely
source_dialogue.text: I doubt it, but it's worth a try I guess.

source_dialogue.speaker: Lottery Official
source_dialogue.text: Absolutely. Even if we don't find the ticket, at least we tried.

scene.narrative_en: Mariely was the lucky winner of the lottery, but when he went to collect his prize, he realized he had lost the winning ticket. He was devastated and angry, especially since he knew that there was no way to replace it.

mapped_refs.notes: 전통시장은 식료품을 사는 전통적인 시장이다
Korean speakers use 번화 to refer to the bustling main commercial street.

source_dialogue.speaker: Abilene
source_dialogue.text: Hey, Pedro. What's up?

source_dialogue.speaker: Pedro
source_dialogue.text: Not much. Just working on some new invention ideas. What are you up to?

source_dialogue.speaker: Abilene
source_dialogue.text: I'm working on a new invention too. I'm trying to create a device that would allow people to communicate with each other without speaking.

source_dialogue.speaker: Pedro
source_dialogue.text: That sounds really interesting! I'd love to hear more about it.

source_dialogue.speaker: Abilene
source_dialogue.text: Well, I'm still in the early stages of development, but I'm hoping that it will be able to send and receive messages using a form of telepathy.

source_dialogue.speaker: Pedro
source_dialogue.text: That's amazing! I can't wait to see it when it's finished.

speakers.speech_style_notes: Measured, inquisitive, uses technical terms casually.

scene.narrative_en: Abilene was working on a new invention. He was trying to create a device that would allow people to communicate with each other without speaking.

mapped_refs.notes: Widely used loanword for telepathy in Korean media.

source_dialogue.speaker: Brittanie
source_dialogue.text: I'm almost done. I just have to dry the dishes and put them away.

source_dialogue.speaker: Mom
source_dialogue.text: Okay, sounds good. Let me know when you're finished so I can check everything over.

source_dialogue.speaker: Brittanie
source_dialogue.text: Will do!

source_dialogue.speaker: Brittanie
source_dialogue.text: Hey mom, I'm finished with the dishes.

source_dialogue.speaker: Mom
source_dialogue.text: Let me take a look.

source_dialogue.speaker: Brittanie
source_dialogue.text: Okay.

source_dialogue.speaker: Mom
source_dialogue.text: Everything looks good! You did a great job.

scene.narrative_en: Brittanie knows that in order to wash the dishes correctly, he must fill up the sink with hot soapy water. He then proceeds to wash each dish individually, making sure to scrub them clean before rinsing them off in the hot water. Once all of the dishes are clean, Brittanie dries them off with a towel and puts them away in their respective cabinets.

source_dialogue.speaker: Asante
source_dialogue.text: Mom, I know my clothes are muddy, but I had so much fun! Playing in the mud is just so much fun.

source_dialogue.speaker: Mother
source_dialogue.text: Asante, you're going to have to change out of those clothes before you come back inside. You're just going to make a mess if you come inside like that.

source_dialogue.speaker: Mother
source_dialogue.text: No, Asante, your whole outfit is muddy. You need to take everything off and put on something clean.

source_dialogue.speaker: Asante
source_dialogue.text: But Mom! This was so much fun! Can't I just play in the mud for a little longer? Please?

source_dialogue.speaker: Mother
source_dialogue.text: No, Asante. You've been playing in the mud long enough. It's time to come inside and get cleaned up now.

speakers.speech_style_notes: Excited, repetitive, pleading tone with childlike enthusiasm.

scene.narrative_en: Asante was outside playing in the mud, and she got her clothes dirty. She didn't care that she was getting muddy, and she had a lot of fun.

mapped_refs.notes: Kids often say '진흙' when talking about muddy play.

source_dialogue.speaker: Teara
source_dialogue.text: I can't believe I've been waiting in this line for so long. It feels like forever.

source_dialogue.speaker: Person behind her
source_dialogue.text: It's only been five minutes. Relax, it'll be your turn soon.

source_dialogue.speaker: Teara
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, I know. But it feels like forever.

source_dialogue.speaker: Person behind her
source_dialogue.text: I understand how you feel. Time can seem to move very slowly when we're waiting for something, especially if we're not sure how long it's going to take.

speakers.speech_style_notes: Speaks with a slightly impatient, measured tone.
Speaks in a soothing, reassuring manner.

scene.narrative_en: Teara was frustrated because she had been waiting in line for a long time. She looked at her watch and saw that she had only been waiting for five minutes, but it felt like forever.

source_dialogue.speaker: Casper
source_dialogue.text: Why are you crying? What's wrong?

source_dialogue.speaker: Erik
source_dialogue.text: I'm just really upset right now. I can't believe you would yell at me over something so small.

source_dialogue.speaker: Casper
source_dialogue.text: I'm sorry, I didn't mean to make you cry. I was just really angry in the moment. But I shouldn't have taken it out on you like that. Can we please talk about this calmly?

source_dialogue.speaker: Erik
source_dialogue.text: I don't know, Casper. I just feel really hurt right now.

source_dialogue.speaker: Casper
source_dialogue.text: Erik, I'm sorry. Please, can we talk about this? I don't want to fight with you.

source_dialogue.speaker: Erik

source_dialogue.speaker: Shan
source_dialogue.text: I just feel like I'm not good enough, you know? No matter what I do, it's never enough.

source_dialogue.speaker: Counselor
source_dialogue.text: It sounds like you have some pretty negative thoughts about yourself. Can you tell me more about that?

source_dialogue.speaker: Shan
source_dialogue.text: Yeah. I guess I just feel like I'm always falling short, no matter what. Like, I'll work really hard at something and then it turns out bad or someone else does it better than me and it just feels awful.

source_dialogue.speaker: Counselor
source_dialogue.text: That doesn't sound like a very positive way to view yourself or your accomplishments. What do you think might be causing these negative thoughts?

source_dialogue.speaker: Shan
source_dialogue.text: I don't know. Maybe it's just how things have always been for me. Whenever something goes wrong, it feels like it's all my fault somehow. And even when things are going well, I just don't feel good enough somehow.

source_dialogue.speaker: Counselor
source_dialogue.text: That doesn't sound like a very healthy way to live your life. Are you willing to try and change that?

source_dialogue.speaker: Shan
source_dialogue.text: Yes, I think so. It's just really hard sometimes to see myself in a positive light when everything around me seems so negative. But I'm willing to try if it will help me feel better about myself.

source_dialogue.speaker: Counselor
source_dialogue.text: Well, let's start by exploring some of the reasons why you might feel this way. Do you think there are certain situations or events that trigger these negative thoughts?

source_dialogue.speaker: Shan
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, I guess so. Like I said, whenever something goes wrong or I don't meet my own expectations, it really bothers me.

source_dialogue.speaker: Counselor
source_dialogue.text: So it sounds like you're pretty hard on yourself. Can you think of any other times when you might feel this way?

source_dialogue.speaker: Shan
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, I guess any time I'm compared to others or have to compete with someone. It just makes me feel really inferior and not good enough.

source_dialogue.speaker: Counselor
source_dialogue.text: That definitely sounds like a lot of pressure that you're putting on yourself. Would you be willing to try and let go of some of that pressure? Maybe give yourself a break every once in awhile?

source_dialogue.speaker: Shan
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, I think that would help. Just knowing that I don't have to be perfect all the time would take a lot of the stress off.

source_dialogue.speaker: Counselor
source_dialogue.text: That's definitely a good idea. Why don't we try some relaxation techniques to help you let go of some of that tension and stress?

scene.narrative_en: Shan seeks counseling to help address his low self-esteem. In counseling, Shan learns ways to cope with his negative thoughts and begins to see himself in a more positive light. He starts to feel more hopeful about his future and takes pride in his accomplishments.

source_dialogue.text: Yeah, I can understand that. It's tough when you feel like you're not being given a chance to show what you can do. But you have to remember that the coach is making the decisions about who plays and who doesn't based on what he thinks is best for the team. Sometimes that means leaving players on the bench who deserve to be playing. It's not personal, it's just business.

source_dialogue.speaker: Luna
source_dialogue.text: I know that, but it still doesn't make it any easier to deal with. I feel like I'm good enough to be starting every game, but instead I'm stuck riding the bench. It feels like a waste of my time and talent.

source_dialogue.speaker: Alayla
source_dialogue.text: I know how you feel, but you have to trust that the coach knows what he's doing. He's been doing this a lot longer than you have and he has the team's best interests at heart. Just keep working hard in practice and be ready to take your opportunity when it comes.

source_dialogue.speaker: Luna
source_dialogue.text: I guess you're right. Thanks for talking to me, man.

source_dialogue.speaker: Alayla

source_dialogue.speaker: Charla
source_dialogue.text: I'm really glad to be out of the hospital and back home. It was tough being in there, but I'm feeling a lot better now.

source_dialogue.speaker: Doctor
source_dialogue.text: I'm glad to hear that Charla. How are you doing with your medication? Are you still taking it as prescribed?

source_dialogue.text: Yes, I am. And I'm feeling a lot better because of it. My moods are more stable and I'm not having any hallucinations or anything like that anymore. So I guess the medication is working well for me.

source_dialogue.speaker: Doctor
source_dialogue.text: That's great to hear! It sounds like you're really making progress with your treatment. But we need to keep an eye on things and make sure that you continue taking your medication as prescribed and attending therapy sessions regularly. If you start to feel like your symptoms are coming back or worsening, please don't hesitate to reach out to us so we can adjust your treatment plan accordingly.

source_dialogue.speaker: Charla
source_dialogue.text: Okay, I will. Thanks for all your help, doctor.

source_dialogue.speaker: Doctor
source_dialogue.text: You're welcome, Charla. We'll be here to support you every step of the way.

Speaks professionally, uses supportive phrasing, moderate pace.

scene.narrative_en: Charla was released from the psychiatric facility because he no longer met the criteria for involuntary commitment. He had been receiving treatment for bipolar disorder and had been stabilized on medication for several months. His doctor and therapist agreed that he could continue treatment as an outpatient and posed no threat to himself or others.

source_dialogue.text: I wanted the title to be reflective of my experience, and "The Girl Who Was A Soldier" just seemed to fit.

source_dialogue.speaker: Kaelyn
source_dialogue.text: Hi, Officer. I was just in a hit-and-run accident. A car rear-ended me at a stop light and then sped away. I'm feeling really scared and alone right now.

source_dialogue.speaker: Kaelyn

source_dialogue.speaker: Officer
source_dialogue.text: Okay. Well, we'll do our best to find the person who did this. In the meantime, try not to worry too much. These things happen sometimes, unfortunately.

source_dialogue.speaker: Kaelyn
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, I guess you're right. Thanks for talking with me, Officer.

source_dialogue.speaker: Officer
source_dialogue.text: No problem, Kaelyn. We'll catch the person who did this and bring them to justice.

source_dialogue.speaker: Ricco
source_dialogue.text: Hi, Abrianna. I was wondering if I could ask you for some advice on gardening.

source_dialogue.speaker: Abrianna
source_dialogue.text: Of course, Ricco. What would you like to know?

source_dialogue.speaker: Ricco
source_dialogue.text: Well, I've never done it before and I was wondering where to start.

source_dialogue.speaker: Abrianna
source_dialogue.text: The first thing you need to do is prepare the soil. You can do this by tilling the ground and adding some compost or manure to help the plants grow. Once the soil is ready, you can plant the seeds. Make sure to water them regularly and give them plenty of sunlight so they can thrive.

source_dialogue.speaker: Ricco
source_dialogue.text: Thank you, Abrianna. I appreciate your help.

source_dialogue.speaker: Abrianna

Calm, clear, instructive, uses complete sentences.

source_dialogue.text: Thank you, Darrius. It's been a long journey, but I'm finally starting to see some results.

source_dialogue.speaker: Eila
source_dialogue.text: Hey, thanks for taking the time to talk with me. I really appreciate it.

source_dialogue.speaker: Director
source_dialogue.text: Of course, no problem. What can I do for you?

source_dialogue.speaker: Eila
source_dialogue.text: Well, like I said, I want to help out with your organisation. I know what it's like to be homeless and I want to help others who are in that situation.

source_dialogue.speaker: Director
source_dialogue.text: That's great! We always need more volunteers. Are you interested in doing any particular tasks? Cooking, cleaning, interacting with the guests? Let me know and we'll see what we can do.

source_dialogue.speaker: Eila
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, that sounds great. I'll do whatever needs to be done.

source_dialogue.speaker: Director
source_dialogue.text: Wonderful! We'll get you started on some training and then you can start helping out as soon as possible. Thanks for your time, Eila.

Professional, concise, upbeat, uses standard corporate phrasing.

scene.narrative_en: Eila joins an organisation because Eila wants to help people who are homeless. Eila has been homeless himself in the past, and he knows how difficult it can be to get back on your feet. He wants to help others in his situation by providing them with food, clothing, and shelter.

source_dialogue.text: So, what brings you to the library?

source_dialogue.text: That's a great idea! The library has a lot of books on the area. Do you have any particular time period or topic in mind?

source_dialogue.speaker: Elyjah

source_dialogue.speaker: Rebekah
source_dialogue.text: I don't understand why our school has a dress code. It's not fair.

source_dialogue.speaker: Mother
source_dialogue.text: What do you mean, it's not fair? What specifically are you upset about?

source_dialogue.speaker: Rebekah
source_dialogue.text: Well, for one thing, it's sexist. Boys can wear whatever they want but girls have to dress a certain way. It's not right.

source_dialogue.speaker: Mother
source_dialogue.text: I see your point. But the school is just trying to maintain a certain level of decorum and professionalism. They don't want students wearing clothes that are too revealing or inappropriate.

source_dialogue.speaker: Rebekah
source_dialogue.text: But that's not fair! Why should girls have to cover up more than boys? It's not right.

source_dialogue.speaker: Mother
source_dialogue.text: I understand how you feel, but the school has its reasons. Maybe you can talk to your teacher or the principal about changing the dress code.

source_dialogue.speaker: Rebekah
source_dialogue.text: I already did. They said that it's not up for discussion.

source_dialogue.speaker: Mother
source_dialogue.text: Well, maybe there's something else you can do then. Have you thought about starting a petition or writing a letter to the school board?

source_dialogue.speaker: Rebekah
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, I'm actually already working on a petition. I'm going to try and get as many people to sign it as possible.

source_dialogue.speaker: Mother
source_dialogue.text: That sounds like a great idea! I'm proud of you for standing up for what you believe in.

scene.narrative_en: Rebekah is a brave little girl. She is always protesting against something she believes is unfair. For example, she recently protested against her school's dress code.

source_dialogue.text: I'm not happy with the service I've been receiving. The coverage is bad and I've been dropped calls.

source_dialogue.speaker: Conner
source_dialogue.text: Doctor, I'm finally feeling better and I really want to be able to move again. I've been bedridden for weeks and I'm extremely weak, but I'm determined to get better. Can you tell me what my prognosis is?

source_dialogue.text: Well, Conner, with time and rehabilitation, you should be able to regain most of your mobility. However, it's important to realistic about your expectations. You may not ever be able to return to your previous level of activity. But if you're willing to put in the work, you can make significant progress.

source_dialogue.speaker: Conner
source_dialogue.text: I'm willing to do whatever it takes. I just want to be able to move again.

source_dialogue.speaker: Doctor
source_dialogue.text: Well, then let's get started on your road to recovery.

Speaks calmly and professionally, measured tone.

scene.narrative_en: Conner is finally feeling better because she wants to be able to move again. She has been bedridden for weeks and is extremely weak, but she is determined to get better. Her doctor says that with time and rehabilitation, she should be able to regain most of her mobility.

source_dialogue.speaker: Selin

source_dialogue.speaker: Selin
source_dialogue.text: Thank you, Father. I just wanted to do my best to honor God.

source_dialogue.speaker: Selin's father
source_dialogue.text: You certainly did that. I could tell that you were praying with all of your heart. It was very moving.

source_dialogue.speaker: Selin
source_dialogue.text: I'm glad you liked it. I was a little nervous at first, but once I started praying, I felt more confident.

source_dialogue.speaker: Selin's father
source_dialogue.text: That's good. It's always important to pray from the heart.

source_dialogue.speaker: Rylie

source_dialogue.speaker: Brad
source_dialogue.text: What's your problem?

source_dialogue.speaker: Rylie
source_dialogue.text: I don't want to fight with you, so I'm just going to go to my room.

source_dialogue.speaker: Brad
source_dialogue.text: We're not fighting. I just want to talk to you.

source_dialogue.speaker: Rylie
source_dialogue.text: Fine. What do you want to talk about?

source_dialogue.speaker: Rylie

source_dialogue.speaker: Brad
source_dialogue.text: Rylie, come on. We're brothers. We should be able to talk about anything. What's going on with you?

source_dialogue.speaker: Rylie

source_dialogue.speaker: Brad
source_dialogue.text: Rylie, you're not dragging me down. I'm worried about you. We all are.

source_dialogue.speaker: Rylie
source_dialogue.text: There's nothing to worry about. I'm fine. Just leave me alone, okay?

source_dialogue.speaker: Brad
source_dialogue.text: Rylie, please talk to me. What's going on?

source_dialogue.speaker: Oralia
source_dialogue.text: Hey, Harry. What's up?

source_dialogue.speaker: Harry
source_dialogue.text: Not much. Just hanging out. What about you?

source_dialogue.speaker: Oralia
source_dialogue.text: I'm just trying to get better. You know, perfect myself.

source_dialogue.speaker: Harry
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, I hear you. But don't you think you're being a little too hard on yourself? I mean, you're already pretty good.

source_dialogue.speaker: Oralia
source_dialogue.text: Good is not good enough for me, Harry. I want to be great. And in order to be great, I have to be perfect.

source_dialogue.speaker: Harry
source_dialogue.text: But nobody's perfect, Oralia. And even if you were perfect, what would that even mean? Would it make you happy?

source_dialogue.speaker: Oralia
source_dialogue.text: Of course it would make me happy! Being perfect would mean that I'm the best at everything I do. And being the best makes me happy because it means that I've achieved something that others haven't been able to achieve. It means that my hard work has paid off and that I'm doing something right.

source_dialogue.speaker: Harry
source_dialogue.text: But is it really worth all the stress and pressure you're putting on yourself? I mean, you're already a great guy. Why can't you just enjoy that?

source_dialogue.speaker: Oralia
source_dialogue.text: Because I want to be perfect! And being perfect is not easy. It takes a lot of hard work and dedication. But it's worth it to me because it means that I can be the best at everything I do.

source_dialogue.speaker: Harry
source_dialogue.text: Well, I guess that's true. But don't forget to enjoy the journey, Oralia. Life is about more than just being perfect.

scene.narrative_en: Oralia is very confident in his abilities and always strives to be the best. He is constantly seeking ways to improve himself and become perfect. Oralia is never satisfied with simply being good, he always wants to be great.
